evidence: LLDB launch was attempted twice against bugs/protobuf/parse_string_oom/debugging/poc_debug but the local macOS environment lacks debugserver, so runtime state was captured via the required printf fallback. The direct CodedInputStream harness decoded the claimed oversized string length as 1073741824 (0x40000000), then returned read_ok=false with current_position advancing only from 5 to 8 and output_hex=414243, showing that only the 3 available bytes were consumed. A separate overflow probe calling PushLimit(INT_MAX) after skipping 5 bytes produced overflow_limit_after_push=-1 with old limit INT_MAX, matching protobuf's expected overflow-guard behavior rather than an incorrect state bug.
